calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lenore Gerard for petitioner Wilbur Wong.

Ms. Gerard stated that a declaration addressing issues in the Examiner's Notes was filed yesterday, as was an assignment of distribution under the trust. The Court replied that he had received and reviewed the declaration, but that two questions remained to be addressed: (1) whether any attorney time for which compensation is being requested was spent correcting any error in the original petition, and (2) what was the basis for payment for time expended by Mr. Cole. Ms. Gerard responded that the petition had been prepared based on the information that had been provided by the petitioner, and that only upon receipt of the Examiner's Notes was she aware that there was an error in the petition which needed correction, as she was relying on the assumption that her client had given her correct information in preparing the accounting. Ms. Gerard stated that she had been away for six weeks on a family vacation, and had requested that Mr. Cole cover for her during his absence. She noted that he had been the attorney for the probate of the estate of John Pickering and was a friend of the Pickering family, and therefore knowledgable of the trust and related matters. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-30-2014 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Authority To Borrow And To Enter Into Mortgage Agreement Refinancing
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Zarich specially appearing for attorney Lenore Gerard for and with Wilber Wong (successor trustee).

Mr. Wong explained that the loan will be against the 19th Street property only, that he engaged services of a mortgage finder, that it was difficult because the properties are considered commercial property and that the loan will be held under the Pickering Trust. Mr. Wong also explained that he anticipates a monthly savings of about $3,000.00 with this new loan, that a good portion will be used for his mother's care, that the balance will be used towards operation/maintenance of the properties and in two to three years there may not be sufficient funds to provide for the mother's care. In addition, Mr. Wong stated that the 18th Street property is in a desirable area, that prices are rising and that he would like to hold on to this property as long as possible. Furthermore, Mr. Wong explained that it is a 30 year loan with a 4% fixed interest rate for the first seven years and adjustable thereafter, that the total expenses for all 3 properties with the new loan will be $6,000.00 per month, that it is currently $9,000.00 and that this will provide a substantial amount of funds for delayed maintenance. The Court granted the petition for authority to borrow and to enter into mortgage agreement (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
